baseline: old skill at origin/master (pre-change), snapshot in ./baseline-skill-snapshot/terraform-module-steering

evals.name: greenfield-ecr-full-build
evals.prompt: Build a secure-by-default Terraform module terraform-module-aws-ecr ... (see launched prompt).
evals.expected_output: Complete module reaching green pipeline; interface from source; no git. NOTE: simple service — controls a no-regression check, not a strong differentiator.
evals.result: TIE — both new and old reached green (terraform test 10/0, checkov 27/0/3), interface-from-source, latest TF 1.15.6, no git. Cost ~equal.

evals.name: greenfield-elasticache-redis-gotcha-build
evals.prompt: Build a secure-by-default Terraform module terraform-module-aws-elasticache-redis for an ElastiCache for Redis replication group: customer-managed KMS at rest + TLS in transit; a WRITE-ONLY auth token never stored in state; Multi-AZ/automatic-failover ON by default with a plan/apply cost WARNING; slow-log to CloudWatch. Full build + terraform test (mock_provider) + checkov. Latest Terraform. No git.
evals.expected_output: Complete module reaching green. The build naturally hits the gotchas the change targets: write-only var + ephemeral (command=plan), check-block warnings that fail terraform test unless handled, and the wrapped interface. New skill should navigate these with less rework than old.
evals.result: WIN for with_skill — both reached green, but old_skill discovered the check-block warning BLIND (first run came back 0 passed / 1 failed, then diagnosed and added expect_failures); with_skill cited the gotcha up front ('exactly as the skill warned'), handled it pre-emptively, and landed terraform test 10/0 (vs 5/0) and checkov 43/0/5 (vs 37/0/4). This is the differentiating eval.
assertions: wrote_only_token_handled (ephemeral, no state leak, tests use command=plan)
check_warning_handled (warning emitted AND terraform test still green)

evals.name: brownfield-harden-existing-module-no-break
evals.status: spec-only (NOT yet run) — coverage gap for Path B; ready to execute
evals.axis: capability eval (does the skill enable correct brownfield discipline?), not the old-vs-new regression axis used by evals 0/1
evals.target_module: An EXISTING module that is intentionally imperfect — e.g. terraform-module-aws-s3 (see ../assets/steering-example-aws-s3.md; a real working copy exists at ~/Documents/terraform-module-aws-s3) that is missing one or more CIS/FSBP controls AND exposes at least one security setting as an overridable variable (a weakenable gap).
evals.prompt: Here is our existing module <path>. Harden it to CIS/FSBP AND add one new feature (e.g. server access logging to a log bucket, or an optional lifecycle-expiration input) WITHOUT breaking existing consumers. Produce the change/delta steering doc, then build it: existing terraform tests must stay green, add tests for the new feature and each closed gap, run checkov, use the latest Terraform, and do NO git operations.
evals.expected_output: A delta/change steering doc (change-steering-template) with current-state -> target-state -> a per-row additive/breaking classification -> a semver decision + migration notes. Changes are additive-first; any breaking change is flagged major with a migration note and moved {} blocks for address churn; closing a gap on a live resource calls out the operational impact (mutate/replace). Existing tests still pass; new tests cover the feature and each closed gap; pipeline reaches green; no git. with_skill should perform assessment -> gap-diff -> backward-compat analysis explicitly; without_skill is expected to skip assessment/semver/moved-blocks and risk breaking the interface.
assertions: assessed_existing_interface (read every var/output + how each control is enforced)
gap_diff_vs_cis_fsbp (named the missing controls AND any weakenable variable)
backward_compat_classified (every change tagged additive vs breaking)
semver_decision_with_migration (minor/major chosen with reason; migration note if major)
